bio.scholar: Barack Obama, the forty-fourth President, made history as the first African American to hold the nation's highest office. The son of a white American mother and Black Kenyan father, Obama graduated from Columbia University and Harvard Law School, where he was the first African American president of the Harvard Law Review. He signed the Affordable Care Act, expanding healthcare access to millions, ordered the operation that killed Osama bin Laden, and navigated the country through the Great Recession of 2008-2009.
bio.explorer: Barack Obama made history as the first African American president of the United States! He grew up in Hawaii and went to Columbia University and Harvard Law School. As the forty-fourth president, he signed a law that helped millions more Americans get health insurance, ordered the mission that found Osama bin Laden, and helped the country recover from a terrible economic crash.

text.scholar: Made history as the first African American president of the United States, shattering a racial barrier that had stood for over two centuries.
text.explorer: He was the first Black president in American history — a huge moment for the country and the world.

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events

text.scholar: Signed the Affordable Care Act (2010), expanding healthcare access to millions of previously uninsured Americans.
text.explorer: He signed a law that helped millions of Americans who didn't have health insurance finally get covered.

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events

text.scholar: Ordered the military operation that killed Osama bin Laden on May 2, 2011, bringing to justice the mastermind of the September 11 attacks.
text.explorer: He ordered the mission that found and stopped Osama bin Laden, the man behind the 9/11 attacks.

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events

text.scholar: Obama was the first African American president of the Harvard Law Review before entering politics, and his 2004 Democratic Convention speech launched him to national prominence.
text.explorer: Before becoming president, he gave such an amazing speech at the 2004 Democratic Convention that everyone in the country noticed him.

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events

text.scholar: He was the son of a white American mother from Kansas and a Black Kenyan father, and grew up in Hawaii and Indonesia.
text.explorer: His mom was from Kansas and his dad was from Kenya. He grew up in Hawaii — the only president born there!

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events

text.scholar: The first official presidential portrait taken with a digital camera was Obama's, in January 2009.
text.explorer: He was the first president whose official photo was taken with a digital camera instead of film!

sources.citation: Miller Center: barack obama - Key Events
